channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Why are people attacking Ebola clinics? It revolves around trust, death and body bags
item.description: In echoes of past outbreaks, community members are attacking clinics, distrusting doctors and following burial traditions that could lead to more cases of Ebola.
item.content:encoded:
In echoes of past outbreaks, community members are attacking clinics, distrusting doctors and following burial traditions that could lead to more cases of Ebola.

(Image credit: Gradel Muyisa Mumbere)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Photos show the world's 'uncounted' people -- and what it takes to be counted
item.description: Colombian-American photographer and filmmaker Juan Arredondo turns his lens on the people of the world who do not have birth and death certificates — and how these vital records are created.
item.content:encoded:
Colombian-American photographer and filmmaker Juan Arredondo turns his lens on the people of the world who do not have birth and death certificates — and how these vital records are created.

(Image credit: Juan Arredondo)
item.dc:creator: Diane Cole

item.title: The U.S. government wants to treat any Americans with Ebola outside of the U.S.
item.description: The U.S. plans to try to keep Ebola out by keeping citizens who were potentially exposed in Kenya. This has some in Kenya frustrated and others worried it will deter aid workers from helping.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. plans to try to keep Ebola out by keeping citizens who were potentially exposed in Kenya. This has some in Kenya frustrated and others worried it will deter aid workers from helping.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: American Cancer Society says adults should start colorectal cancer screenings at 45
item.description: The American Cancer Society is changing its screening recommendations for colorectal cancer, suggesting adults begin screening at age 45 and adding blood-based tests as an option.
item.content:encoded: The American Cancer Society is changing its screening recommendations for colorectal cancer, suggesting adults begin screening at age 45 and adding blood-based tests as an option.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak is spiraling, with health workers struggling to contain the virus
item.description: The epicenter of the Ebola outbreak is in Mongbwalu, a poor gold-mining town of 130,000 people, in Ituri province, in eastern Congo.
item.content:encoded:
The epicenter of the Ebola outbreak is in Mongbwalu, a poor gold-mining town of 130,000 people, in Ituri province, in eastern Congo.

(Image credit: Michel Lunanga)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: Therapists are using AI to take notes. Is it a useful tool or a breach of trust?
item.description: New companies are selling artificial intelligence assistance to mental health therapists. The AI tools can help with administration and recordkeeping, but some patients worry about their privacy.
item.content:encoded:
New companies are selling artificial intelligence assistance to mental health therapists. The AI tools can help with administration and recordkeeping, but some patients worry about their privacy.

(Image credit: Fiordaliso)
item.dc:creator: Windsor Johnston

item.title: Attacks from residents complicate the fight against a rare type of Ebola
item.description: Three times in the past week, healthcare facilities have been attacked. On Sunday, angry young men stormed a hospital treating Ebola patients, forcing medical staff to evacuate them as gunfire rang out.
item.content:encoded:
Three times in the past week, healthcare facilities have been attacked. On Sunday, angry young men stormed a hospital treating Ebola patients, forcing medical staff to evacuate them as gunfire rang out.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In Beirut, refugee girls and women learn more than self-defense in martial arts class
item.description: In the male-dominated world of Palestinian refugee camps in Lebanon, a martial arts teacher offers women a way to empowerment.
item.content:encoded:
In the male-dominated world of Palestinian refugee camps in Lebanon, a martial arts teacher offers women a way to empowerment.

(Image credit: Aline Deschamps for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: COMIC: To raise a confident little talker, learn to speak 'parentese'
item.description: So you've got a baby in your life. How do you talk to them, exactly? In this comic, experts explain why conversing with your baby is important — and share ways to help them develop their language skills.
item.content:encoded: So you've got a baby in your life. How do you talk to them, exactly? In this comic, experts explain why conversing with your baby is important — and share ways to help them develop their language skills.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: Spin to win: How this life insurance company turns healthy habits into a game
item.description: You've heard of lower car insurance rates for good drivers. Now, one life insurance company wants to incentivize longevity with lower rates and extra bonuses for people who adopt healthy habits.
item.content:encoded:
You've heard of lower car insurance rates for good drivers. Now, one life insurance company wants to incentivize longevity with lower rates and extra bonuses for people who adopt healthy habits.

(Image credit: manusapon kasosod/Moment RF)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey
